name: dum-dum-irc

metadata.name: dum-dum-irc
metadata.summary: A non-standard internet relay chat program.

documentation:
# dum-dum-irc

[![](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/dum-dum-irc?style=flat-square)](https://pypi.org/project/dum-dum-irc/)
[![](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/thegamecracks/dum-dum-irc/pyright-lint.yml?style=flat-square&label=pyright)](https://microsoft.github.io/pyright/#/)
[![](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/thegamecracks/dum-dum-irc/python-test.yml?style=flat-square&logo=pytest&label=tests)](https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/)

A handcrafted implementation of an internet relay chat without following
any conventions or RFC standards.

![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thegamecracks/dum-dum-irc/main/docs/images/demo.png)

### [Changelog] | [Contributing Guide]

[Changelog]: https://github.com/thegamecracks/dum-dum-irc/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md
[Contributing Guide]: https://github.com/thegamecracks/dum-dum-irc/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

## Usage

With Python 3.11+ installed, you can get the current stable version on PyPI:

```sh
pip install dum-dum-irc
```

Or if you have Git, you can install the latest in-development version:

```sh
pip install git+https://github.com/thegamecracks/dum-dum-irc
```

Once installed, you can use the `dumdum` and `dumdum-server` commands.
Running `dumdum` starts a graphical client which you can use to join
Dumdum servers, while `dumdum-server` is used to host servers of your own.
Both support the `-h/--help` option if you want to see more details:

```sh
usage: dumdum [-h] [-v] {appdirs} ...

Start the client interface for connecting to dumdum servers.

positional arguments:
  {appdirs}

options:
  -h, --help     show this help message and exit
  -v, --verbose  Increase logging verbosity
```

```sh
usage: dumdum-server [-h] [-v] [-c CHANNELS [CHANNELS ...]] [--host HOST] [--port PORT] [--cert CERT] [--max-messages MAX_MESSAGES]

Host a dumdum server.

To use TLS encryption, you must provide a certificate and private key.
This can be specified as either:
1. A single file containing both the private key and certificate:
     --cert hello.pem
2. A pair of certificate and private key files, separated with a colon:
     --cert hello.crt:hello.key

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  -v, --verbose         Increase logging verbosity
  -c CHANNELS [CHANNELS ...], --channels CHANNELS [CHANNELS ...]
                        A list of channels
  --host HOST           The address to host on, or all interfaces when not supplied
  --port PORT           The port number to host on (default: 6667)
  --cert CERT           The SSL certificate and private key to use
  --max-messages MAX_MESSAGES
                        The maximum number of messages cached per channel (default: 1000)
```

## Implementation

Dumdum consists of two parts:

1. The Sans-IO protocol, defined in [dumdum.protocol]
2. The asyncio wrapper, defined in [dumdum.client] and [dumdum.server]

The [Sans-IO] protocol is responsible for handling the generation and
consumption of byte streams, along with producing events from received
messages, while the asyncio wrapper is responsible for the actual network
communication between the server and its clients.

[Sans-IO]: https://sans-io.readthedocs.io/

[dumdum.protocol]: https://github.com/thegamecracks/dum-dum-irc/tree/main/src/dumdum/protocol
[dumdum.client]: https://github.com/thegamecracks/dum-dum-irc/tree/main/src/dumdum/client
[dumdum.server]: https://github.com/thegamecracks/dum-dum-irc/tree/main/src/dumdum/server

## Protocol

Clients are able to send the following messages:

1. HELLO: `0x00 | 1-byte version`
2. AUTHENTICATE: `0x02 | varchar nickname (32)`
3. SEND_MESSAGE: `0x03 | varchar channel name (32) | varchar content (1024)`
4. LIST_CHANNELS: `0x04`
5. LIST_MESSAGES: `0x05 | 8-byte before snowflake or 0 | 8-byte after snowflake or 0`

Servers are able to send the following messages:

1. HELLO: `0x00 | 0 or 1 using SSL`
2. INCOMPATIBLE_VERSION: `0x01 | 1-byte version`
3. ACKNOWLEDGE_AUTHENTICATION: `0x02 | 0 or 1 success`
4. SEND_MESSAGE: `0x03 | 8-byte snowflake | varchar channel name (32) | varchar nickname (32) | varchar content (1024)`
5. LIST_CHANNELS: `0x04 | 2-byte length | varchar channel name (32) | ...`
6. LIST_MESSAGES: `0x05 | 3-byte length | same fields after SEND_MESSAGE | ...`

Clients must send a HELLO command and wait for the server to respond with HELLO.
Afterwards the client must send an AUTHENTICATE command and wait for a successful
ACKNOWLEDGE_AUTHENTICATION before they can begin chat communications.

When the client disconnects and reconnects, they MUST re-send hello
and re-authenticate with the server.

If the server supports SSL, they can set `using SSL` in HELLO to indicate
that the client should upgrade the connection to SSL.
At this point, the protocol should not receive any data until after the
SSL handshake is complete.

As this protocol has been intentionally designed to be simple (no timeouts
or keep alives), I/O wrappers do not need a significant amount of work to
implement it.

## TLS Encryption

`dumdum-server` can use SSL certificates to encrypt connections.
For example, if you want to use a self-signed certificate and private key,
you can install OpenSSL and run the following command:

```sh
openssl req -new -x509 -days 365 -noenc -out dumdum.crt -keyout dumdum.key
```

After filling out (or skipping) the certificate signing request prompts,
you can then run the server with the resulting `dumdum.crt` and `dumdum.key`
files:

```sh
dumdum-server --cert dumdum.crt:dumdum.key
```

As for the `dumdum` client, SSL can be enabled by the user before connecting
to a server. If the server uses a self-signed certificate, the user will need
to download the server's certificate (.crt) and select it.

modules.name: dumdum.server.__main__
documentation:
Host a dumdum server.

To use TLS encryption, you must provide a certificate and private key.
This can be specified as either:
1. A single file containing both the private key and certificate:
     --cert hello.pem
2. A pair of certificate and private key files, separated with a colon:
     --cert hello.crt:hello.key

classes.name: dumdum.client.errors.ClientCannotUpgradeSSLError
documentation: Raised when the server requires SSL and the client cannot upgrade.

classes.name: dumdum.client.errors.ServerCannotUpgradeSSLError
documentation: Raised when the server does not use SSL and the client requires it.

classes.name: dumdum.client.errors.AuthenticationFailedError
documentation: Raised when authentication with the dumdum server fails.

classes.name: dumdum.client.errors.DisconnectRequested
documentation: Raised when the current connection should be disconnected.

classes.name: dumdum.client.event_thread.EventThread
documentation: Handles starting and stopping an asyncio event loop in a separate thread.

class_variables.name: dumdum.client.app.TkApp.client
documentation: NOTE: only assigned in attempt_connection()

modules.name: dumdum.client.__main__
documentation: Start the client interface for connecting to dumdum servers.

classes.name: dumdum.protocol.errors.ProtocolError
documentation: The base class for errors related to the protocol.

classes.name: dumdum.protocol.errors.BufferOverflowError
documentation:
The protocol received more data than the buffer could store.

This may be the result of the buffer size being set too low,
or possibly malformed data that the protocol is unable to parse.

Clients and servers, upon receiving this error, should immediately
terminate their connection.

classes.name: dumdum.protocol.errors.MalformedDataError
documentation:
Raised when there is unambiguously malformed data.

Clients and servers, upon receiving this error, should immediately
terminate their connection.

classes.name: dumdum.protocol.errors.InvalidLengthError
documentation: An invalid length was specified for a variable-length message.

classes.name: dumdum.protocol.errors.InvalidStateError
documentation: Some action cannot be handled in the protocol's current state.

classes.name: dumdum.protocol.server.events.ServerEvent
documentation: An event received by the server.

classes.name: dumdum.protocol.server.events.ServerEventHello
documentation: The client says hello.

classes.name: dumdum.protocol.server.events.ServerEventIncompatibleVersion
documentation: The client tried using an incompatible protocol version.

classes.name: dumdum.protocol.server.events.ServerEventAuthentication
documentation: The client attempted to authenticate with the server.

classes.name: dumdum.protocol.server.events.ServerEventMessageReceived
documentation: The client sent a message to the server.

classes.name: dumdum.protocol.server.events.ServerEventListChannels
documentation: The client requested a list of channels.

classes.name: dumdum.protocol.server.events.ServerEventListMessages
documentation: The client requested a list of messages.

classes.name: dumdum.protocol.client.events.ClientEvent
documentation: An event received by the client.

classes.name: dumdum.protocol.client.events.ClientEventHello
documentation: The server responded to our hello.

classes.name: dumdum.protocol.client.events.ClientEventIncompatibleVersion
documentation: The server does not support our protocol version.

classes.name: dumdum.protocol.client.events.ClientEventAuthentication
documentation: The server responded to our authentication request.

classes.name: dumdum.protocol.client.events.ClientEventMessageReceived
documentation: The server broadcasted a message to the client.

classes.name: dumdum.protocol.client.events.ClientEventChannelsListed
documentation: The server responded to our request for a channel list.

classes.name: dumdum.protocol.client.events.ClientEventMessagesListed
documentation: The server responded to our request for a message list.

modules.name: dumdum.protocol.snowflake
documentation:
64 63                                           19     12           0
 0  00011000111000111000000101110101101101000100 1110101 000000000111

64-63: Always 0
63-19: Unix timestamp in milliseconds
19-12: Process ID
12-00: Incrementing per-process ID

classes.name: dumdum.protocol.client.protocol.Client
documentation: The client connected to a server.

classes.name: dumdum.protocol.server.protocol.Server
documentation: The server for a single client.

methods.name: dumdum.protocol.interfaces.Protocol.receive_bytes
documentation: Receive bytes from the sender.

methods.name: dumdum.db.connection.Connection.execute
documentation: Execute query and return a cursor.

methods.name: dumdum.db.connection.Connection.executescript
documentation: Execute one or more SQL statements.

methods.name: dumdum.db.connection.Connection.transaction
documentation: Returns a context manager to begin a transaction.

methods.name: dumdum.db.connection.Connection.fetchone
documentation:
Execute query and return the first row.

If the results are empty, None is returned.

methods.name: dumdum.db.connection.Connection.fetchall
documentation:
Execute query and return all rows.

If the results are empty, None is returned.

methods.name: dumdum.db.connection.Connection.fetchval
documentation:
Execute query and return the first value of the first row.

If the results are empty, None is returned.
